User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the PassMark platform, including students, tutors, admins, and campus representatives.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

fullName.description: The full name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user, used for authentication.

role.description: The role of the user within the platform (student, tutor, admin, or campus_rep).

university.description: The name of the university the user is associated with.

department.description: The name of the department the user is associated with.

subscriptionStatus.description: The current subscription status of the user (free, active, or premium).

subscriptionExpiry.description: The date and time when the user's current subscription expires.

referralCode.description: A unique code assigned to the user for referring new users.

referredBy.description: The referral code of the user who referred this user, if any.

University.title: University
University.description: Represents a university supported by the PassMark platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the University entity.

name.description: The official name of the university.

state.description: The state in Nigeria where the university is located.

Department.title: Department
Department.description: Represents a department within a university, organizing courses.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Department entity.

universityId.description: Reference to the University this department belongs to. (Relationship: University 1:N Department)

name.description: The official name of the department.

Course.title: Course
Course.description: Represents a course offered within a specific university department.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Course entity.

universityId.description: Reference to the University offering this course. (Relationship: University 1:N Course)

departmentId.description: Reference to the Department offering this course. (Relationship: Department 1:N Course)

courseCode.description: The unique code for the course (e.g., 'CSC101').

courseTitle.description: The full title of the course.

PastQuestion.title: PastQuestion
PastQuestion.description: Represents a past question paper uploaded to the platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PastQuestion entity.

universityId.description: Reference to the University this past question belongs to. (Relationship: University 1:N PastQuestion)

departmentId.description: Reference to the Department this past question belongs to. (Relationship: Department 1:N PastQuestion)

courseId.description: Reference to the Course this past question is for. (Relationship: Course 1:N PastQuestion)

year.description: The academic year the past question paper was used.

fileURL.description: The URL to the PDF file of the past question stored in Firebase Storage.

verified.description: Indicates if the past question has been reviewed and verified by an admin.

Tutor.title: Tutor
Tutor.description: Represents a tutor's profile and details, extending from a User entity.

userId.description: Unique identifier for the Tutor entity, also serving as a reference to the associated User. (Relationship: User 1:1 Tutor)

bio.description: A short biography of the tutor.

hourlyRate.description: The tutor's hourly rate for booking sessions.

coursesTaughtIds.description: An array of Course IDs that the tutor teaches. (Relationship: Tutor N:N Course)

rating.description: The average rating received by the tutor from students.

approved.description: Indicates if the tutor's profile has been approved by an admin.

Booking.title: Booking
Booking.description: Represents a tutoring session booking made by a student with a tutor.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Booking entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the User (student) who made the booking. (Relationship: User 1:N Booking)

tutorId.description: Reference to the User (tutor) booked for the session. (Relationship: User 1:N Booking)

courseId.description: Reference to the Course for which the booking is made. (Relationship: Course 1:N Booking)

date.description: The scheduled date and time for the tutoring session.

status.description: The current status of the booking (e.g., pending, confirmed, completed, cancelled).

paymentStatus.description: The payment status for the booking (e.g., pending, paid, refunded).

Subscription.title: Subscription
Subscription.description: Represents a user's subscription to a PassMark plan.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Subscription entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this subscription. (Relationship: User 1:N Subscription)

plan.description: The type of subscription plan (standard or premium).

amount.description: The amount paid for the subscription.

semester.description: The academic semester for which the subscription is valid (e.g., 'Harmattan 2023').

status.description: The current status of the subscription (e.g., active, expired, cancelled).

startDate.description: The date and time when the subscription became active.

endDate.description: The date and time when the subscription is set to expire.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The document ID `{userId}` matches the `request.auth.uid`. Includes `role`, `subscriptionStatus`, and other personal user data. Access is strictly self-owned (user can read/write their own document) or admin-controlled. The `id` field in the document MUST match `{userId}`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: A collection used for database-backed access control (DBAC) to identify admin users. The document ID `{userId}` corresponds to an admin's UID. The mere existence of a document at this path grants admin privileges. Write access only for existing Admins.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who is an admin, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: A collection used for database-backed access control (DBAC) to identify campus representative users. The document ID `{userId}` corresponds to a campus rep's UID. The mere existence of a document at this path grants campus rep privileges. Write access only for Admins.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who is a campus representative, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores details for each university. Publicly readable by all authenticated users. Write access is restricted to Admins. The `id` field in the document MUST match `{universityId}`.

params.name: universityId
params.description: The unique ID of the university.

definition.description: Stores departments within a specific university. The `universityId` field is denormalized within each `Department` document for Authorization Independence and QAPs. Publicly readable by all authenticated users. Write access is restricted to Admins. The `id` field in the document MUST match `{departmentId}`.

params.name: universityId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent university.

params.name: departmentId
params.description: The unique ID of the department.

definition.description: Stores courses offered within a specific department. Both `universityId` and `departmentId` fields are denormalized within each `Course` document for Authorization Independence and QAPs. Publicly readable by all authenticated users. Write access is restricted to Admins. The `id` field in the document MUST match `{courseId}`.

params.name: universityId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent university.

params.name: departmentId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent department.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique ID of the course.

definition.description: Stores past question papers. `universityId`, `departmentId`, `courseId` are denormalized for Authorization Independence and QAPs. Student read access is granted only if the user is an active subscriber and the `verified` field is `true`. Write (upload/edit) access is restricted to Admins. The `id` field in the document MUST match `{pastQuestionId}`.

params.name: universityId
params.description: The unique ID of the university associated with the past question.

params.name: departmentId
params.description: The unique ID of the department associated with the past question.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique ID of the course associated with the past question.

params.name: pastQuestionId
params.description: The unique ID of the past question paper.

definition.description: Stores tutor profiles. The `userId` field in the document MUST match `{tutorId}`. Publicly readable if `approved` is `true`. Tutors can read and update their own profiles (except the `approved` status). Admins have full read/write access. The `approved` field is crucial for QAPs, enabling secure listing of only approved tutors.

params.name: tutorId
params.description: The unique ID of the tutor, which is also their `userId`.

definition.description: Stores tutoring session bookings. `studentId` and `tutorId` are denormalized for Authorization Independence. Students can read/write their own bookings, and Tutors can read/write bookings where they are the tutor. Admins have full access. The `id` field in the document MUST match `{bookingId}`.

params.name: bookingId
params.description: The unique ID of the booking.

definition.description: Stores individual user subscriptions. The `userId` field is denormalized within the `Subscription` document and MUST match `{userId}` in the path. Access is strictly self-owned (user can read/write their own subscription document) or admin-controlled. The `id` field in the document MUST match `{subscriptionId}`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns this subscription.

params.name: subscriptionId
params.description: The unique ID of the subscription.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and debuggable, strictly adhering to the core design principles and strategic mandates. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** Authorization context is denormalized across all relevant documents. For instance, `Department` documents explicitly contain `universityId`, `Course` documents contain both `universityId` and `departmentId`, `PastQuestion` documents contain `universityId`, `departmentId`, and `courseId`. Similarly, `Booking` documents include `studentId` and `tutorId`, and `Subscription` documents include `userId`. This ensures that security rules can make authorization decisions based solely on the `resource.data` of the document being accessed, or by comparing `request.auth.uid` directly to fields within the document, without requiring costly and complex `get()` calls to parent documents. This design allows for atomic operations and simplifies rule logic considerably.

**Clarity of Intent & Structural Segregation (Debuggability & QAPs):**

1.  **User Data:** `/users/{userId}` is a top-level collection where each user's profile is stored. Access is restricted to the owning user (`request.auth.uid == userId`) or an Admin. User roles (`role` field) and subscription status (`subscriptionStatus`) are stored here, enabling direct checks for read/write permissions on other entities.
2.  **Global Roles:** Dedicated collections like `/roles_admin/{userId}` and `/roles_campus_rep/{userId}` are used. These collections leverage the 'existence over content' principle for DBAC. To check if a user is an admin, rules can simply use `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))`. This is highly efficient and secure for role-based access checks, directly supporting QAPs by allowing clear, non-filtering rule conditions.
3.  **Hierarchical Public Data:** Collections like `/universities`, `/universities/{universityId}/departments`, and `/universities/{universityId}/departments/{departmentId}/courses` maintain a logical hierarchy. However, each sub-entity document (Department, Course) explicitly denormalizes its parent IDs (`universityId`, `departmentId`) into its own fields. This ensures that a `list` operation on, say, `courses` can check `resource.data.universityId` without needing to fetch the university document, thus enabling secure QAPs where read access is public but write access is restricted (e.g., to Admins).
4.  **Content-Gated Data (Past Questions):** `PastQuestion` documents are located at `/universities/{universityId}/departments/{departmentId}/courses/{courseId}/pastQuestions/{pastQuestionId}`. They include `universityId`, `departmentId`, `courseId`, and a `verified` boolean. Student access to download past questions is strictly controlled by checking if the `PastQuestion` document has `verified: true` AND if the requesting user has an 'active' or 'premium' `subscriptionStatus` (read from their `/users/{userId}` document). This combination of in-document status and user profile status (checked via `get` on the user's *own* document, which is safe) directly enables QAPs for `list` operations; students can only retrieve verified, relevant past questions if they are subscribed. Admin access to upload/manage is separate.
5.  **Tutor Profiles:** `/tutors/{tutorId}` stores tutor-specific information. The `approved` boolean field within each `Tutor` document determines public visibility. Students can `list` and `read` only approved tutor profiles (`where('approved', '==', true)`), while tutors can always read/write their own profile (except for `approved` status), and Admins have full access. This ensures secure `list` queries without complex filtering logic in rules.
6.  **Bookings:** `/bookings/{bookingId}` documents contain `studentId` and `tutorId`. This allows secure access for both parties involved in a booking, as well as Admins, without requiring complex lookups or `get()` operations. Rules will simply check `request.auth.uid` against these fields.
7.  **Subscriptions:** `/users/{userId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}` is a subcollection of the user, clearly indicating ownership. The `userId` is also denormalized within the `Subscription` document. This allows for simple, path-based ownership rules, where only the subscribing user or an Admin can manage their subscriptions.

This structure prioritizes flat, self-contained documents where authorization context is embedded, allowing for simpler, more robust, and highly performant Firestore security rules and efficient data retrieval, especially for `list` operations.
